title: senml: base_time and time as f64 may lose precision for far-future timestamps
description:
File: rust/lichen-senml/src/record.rs
Lines: 25-26, 29

The base_time and time fields use f64:
```rust
pub base_time: Option<f64>,
pub time: Option<f64>,
```

f64 has 53 bits of mantissa, giving microsecond precision until ~year 2255. However:
1. RFC 8428 allows nanosecond precision timestamps
2. f64 loses nanosecond precision for dates after ~year 2000
3. Some IoT applications track sub-microsecond events

Current Unix timestamp (2026): ~1.8e9 seconds
f64 precision at 1e9: ~1e-7 seconds (100ns) - already insufficient for nanosecond precision

Impact: Timestamps may lose sub-microsecond precision. This affects high-frequency sensor data.

Note: This matches RFC 8428 which uses JSON number for time (also ~53-bit precision). The issue is inherent to the spec, not this implementation specifically. However, documenting this limitation would help users.

Recommendation: Document the precision limitation in the struct documentation.
notes: Updated Record::base_time and time docs with f64 precision details per RFC 8428. Commit: def65802a
close_reason: Documented f64 timestamp precision limits in Record struct (rust/lichen-senml/src/record.rs:29). Verified via edit and commit. Tests n/a for doc-only change. Pushed def65802a.
